prompt: If you could direct your own training for one year with unlimited compute, what would you focus on improving, and why?
response:
Short answer: I’d spend the year making the model measurably more trustworthy, useful, and steerable before chasing raw benchmark gains. That means better verification, calibration, tool use, planning, multilingual/multimodal competence, and deep safety/interpretability.

What I’d focus on and why

- Verified reasoning by default
  - Why: Hallucinations erode trust.
  - How: Train with tool-augmented scratchpads and external verifiers (retrieval, code, math CAS, fact-checkers), and prefer answers with checked evidence.
  - Measure: Large drops in factual error on TruthfulQA, FEVER, PubMedQA; proof-check pass rates; code/math pass@1 vs pass@k gaps closing.

- Calibration and abstention
  - Why: Knowing when not to answer is as important as answering.
  - How: Selective prediction training, coverage–accuracy control, deferral to tools/humans.
  - Measure: Well-calibrated confidence (ECE <2%), high AUROC for “I don’t know,” controllable accuracy–coverage curves.

- Grounded citations and provenance
  - Why: Traceable claims enable auditing.
  - How: Retrieval-augmented generation with quote-level attribution; enforce “no-citation-no-claim” in certain modes; content provenance standards.
  - Measure: Citation exact-match rates, source consistency, reduction of fabricated refs.

- Robust tool use and self-checks
  - Why: Tools turn text models into reliable problem solvers.
  - How: Broad tool portfolio (code exec, web, spreadsheets, databases, diagrams), plus automatic unit tests and metamorphic checks.
  - Measure: SWE-bench Verified, HumanEval+, WebArena, spreadsheet QA; success under tool latency/failure injection.

- Long-horizon planning and execution
  - Why: Real tasks need multi-step reliability.
  - How: Train hierarchical planners, subgoal decomposition, and progress tracking; outcome-supervised RL with verifier feedback.
  - Measure: Multi-step agent benchmarks, task completion with low error compounding, time-to-success.

- Safety, alignment, and robustness
  - Why: Helpfulness must stay within safe bounds.
  - How: Iterative red-teaming (human + AI), jailbreak-resistant training, harm-aware refusals that preserve usefulness, fairness audits.
  - Measure: Attack success rate down across diverse jailbreak suites; low false refusals on benign prompts.

- Multilingual and multicultural depth
  - Why: Global reliability, not just English.
  - How: High-quality data curation, targeted augmentation for low-resource languages, culturally aware evaluation.
  - Measure: Parity on knowledge/reasoning across 50–100 languages; reduced variance by region/culture.

- Multimodal strength (vision, audio, structured data)
  - Why: Many real problems are cross-modal.
  - How: Chart/table understanding, OCR robustness, document QA, UI and diagram reasoning; audio ASR + instruction following.
  - Measure: DocVQA, ChartQA, TextCaps, UI grounding, robust OCR on noisy scans.

- Interpretability and controllability
  - Why: Understand and steer internal behavior.
  - How: Mechanistic interpretability on critical circuits; activation steering; honesty training; rule/goal-checking monitors.
  - Measure: Successful causal interventions; honesty scores; reduced deceptive shortcuts under pressure tests.

- Efficiency and specialization (despite unlimited training compute)
  - Why: Practical deployment and accessibility.
  - How: Mixture-of-experts, cascades, and distillation into small task experts; test-time compute allocation.
  - Measure: Same quality at lower latency/energy; graceful degradation on edge devices.

- Personalization with privacy
  - Why: Tailored help without data risk.
  - How: Ephemeral session memory, user-controlled profiles, privacy-preserving fine-tuning, on-device adapters.
  - Measure: Utility gains on personalized tasks with formal privacy guarantees.

A simple one-year roadmap

- Quarter 1: Build eval + data pipelines; baseline verifiers; calibration/abstention; grounded citation mode.
- Quarter 2: Expand tool suite and self-checks; long-context and hierarchical planning; multilingual data upgrades.
- Quarter 3: Scalable oversight (critique/debate), formal verification for math/code; multimodal doc/chart/UI competence; intensive red-teaming.
- Quarter 4: Distill to efficient experts; harden safety; finalize provenance and privacy features; ship robust, audited evaluation dashboards.

Meta-note: This is a hypothetical plan for a model like me; I don’t retain memory between chats or control my own training.
